title: Understand: Executive Branch—The Scope and Limits of Presidential Power
description: Check your understanding of the the scopes and limits of presidential power with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Executive Branch—The Scope and Limits of Presidential Power
Understand: Executive Branch—The Scope and Limits of Presidential Power

assessment_items.question_content:
**What power is explicitly granted to the president by the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Declaring war
Regulating interstate commerce
Nominating federal judges
Levying taxes
Orders issued by Congress to the president
Formal orders issued by the president to the House of Representatives
Formal orders issued by the president to direct the operations of the executive branch
Decisions made by the president in consultation with the Supreme Court

assessment_items.question_content:
**Under what circumstances can the president issue a pardon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Only with the approval of Congress
Only for federal crimes, not for state crimes
After a convicted criminal has served their entire sentence
Under any circumstances, without limitations

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the "bully pulpit," as it pertains to the president?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president's ability to veto legislation passed by Congress
The President's power to influence public opinion and shape policy through speeches and media appearances
The president's authority to deploy any branch of the military without congressional approval
The president's role as the commander in chief of the armed forces

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which amendment to the U.S. Constitution limits the president to two terms in office?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Fifteenth Amendment
Twentieth Amendment
Twenty-Second Amendment
Twenty-Fifth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the process for impeaching a president?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president can be impeached only for committing a federal crime.
Impeachment is initiated by a majority vote in the House of Representatives, followed by a trial in the Senate.
Impeachment is initiated by a majority vote in the Senate, followed by a trial in the House of Representatives.
The president can be impeached only by the Supreme Court.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Can the president be impeached for actions taken before assuming office?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes, any actions believed to be unlawful can be grounds for impeachment.
No, impeachment applies only to actions taken while in office.
Yes, but only if those actions directly affect the president's ability to perform their duties.
No, because Congress does not have the power to impeach the president.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the role of the vice president in the event of the president's impeachment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The vice president takes the position of president if the president is removed from office.
The vice president oversees the president's impeachment trial in the Senate.
The vice president takes the position of acting president while the impeachment trial is occurring.
The vice president does not become president under any circumstances.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Can the president be impeached for conduct other than an actual crime?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes, the president can be impeached for many types of offenses, not just crimes.
No, impeachment requires evidence of criminal wrongdoing, even if formal criminal charges haven’t been filed.
Yes, but only if the president’s conduct will likely lead to criminal charges.
No, impeachment is strictly limited to presidents who have been formally convicted of a crime.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Can a former president be elected to the presidency again after already being elected president twice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes, there is no restriction on how many times a former president may be elected to the presidency.
No, the Twenty-Second Amendment prohibits a person from being elected to the presidency more than twice.
Yes, but only after a waiting period of ten years.
No, the Constitution bars former presidents from serving non-consecutive terms.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the main purpose of the presidential pardon power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To grant immunity to those convicted under state laws
To allow the president to overturn Supreme Court decisions
To provide a way for the president to show mercy and forgiveness in appropriate cases
To authorize the president to remove individuals from public office

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the primary responsibility of the president?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To veto laws
To make laws
To interpret laws
To execute laws
